title: Api complexity limit error message/data
excerpt: GraphQL supports returning both the error and data arrays for the same query/mutation, it would be nice when complexity limits are exceeded, if the data object still returned the complexity part of the mutation/query so …

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
GraphQL supports returning both the error and data arrays for the same query/mutation, it would be nice when complexity limits are exceeded, if the data object still returned the complexity part of the mutation/query so we can see when it resets in a structured object.

That would be much easier than having to parse the error message for the info required. Which is the workaround used today.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there @anon29275264!

Thank you for that feedback.

I have added this as a feature request to our team

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: kolaai (alfred)

posts.cookedHtml:
Ah was not aware.

Pretty odd use of a bot.

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml: Found this user while doing some digging in the community - I flagged the post so our team can review it!

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml: @kolaai, I was wondering why “Rachel” was giving such odd answers.
